previous_statement: (Hypotheses verification). Verify that the hypotheses of Nodes 1.1 and 1.2 are satisfied in the setting of the main theorem. Specifically: (i) A is a unital *-algebra and φ ∈ S(A) is a state, so the GNS construction applies and the face-commutant correspondence (Node 1.1) holds. (ii) K = S(A) is a compact convex set (by Banach-Alaoglu), and the functionals f_j(ψ) = ψ(a_j) are continuous affine on S(A) (since a_j ∈ A^{sa} are bounded), so the Weis-Shirokov theorem (Node 1.2) applies with K = S(A) and m constraints.
new_statement: (Hypotheses verification — CORRECTED scope). The main theorem applies to: (A) C*-algebras, (B) Archimedean order unit spaces, and (C) *-algebras equipped with an Archimedean quadratic module M (following Klep-Magron-Volcic / the formally verified framework in af-tests). Specifically: (i) For C*-algebras A and states phi in S(A), the GNS construction yields bounded representations (by the C*-identity ||a*a|| = ||a||^2 giving ||pi(a)|| <= ||a||), so the face-commutant correspondence (Node 1.1) applies. (ii) For *-algebras with Archimedean quadratic module M, M-positive states are uniformly bounded (by Cauchy-Schwarz + Archimedean property: |phi(a)| <= sqrt(N_a) where a*a <= N_a·1 mod M), the state space S_M is compact in the pointwise topology (by Tychonoff), and the GNS construction yields bounded representations. This extends the scope beyond C*-algebras. (iii) The state space S(A) (or S_M) is compact convex in the weak-* (pointwise) topology. Compactness follows from Banach-Alaoglu for C*-algebras, or from Tychonoff + closedness of state conditions for Archimedean *-algebras. (iv) The functionals f_j(psi) = psi(a_j) are continuous affine on S(A) by definition of the weak-* topology (not because a_j is 'bounded' per se). The Weis-Shirokov theorem (Node 1.2) applies with K = S(A) and m constraints. Note: for GENERAL *-algebras without the Archimedean property, the GNS construction may give unbounded operators and the state space need not be compact, so the theorem does not apply in that generality.
